texto: I’d always secretly believed that a love as fierce and true as mine would be rewarded in the end, and now I was being forced to accept the bitter truth.
explicacao: Citação de Alma Katsu sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I’d always secretly believed that a love as fierce and true as mine would be rewarded in the end,…” articula reflexao, hopeful, hopelessness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e hopelessness.

frase_original: I’d always secretly believed that a love as fierce and true as mine would be rewarded in the end, and now I was being forced to accept the bitter truth.

texto: We were arrogant and naive, thinking we knew what we felt then was love. Love can be a cheap emotion, lightly given, thought it didn´t seem so to me at the time. Looking back, I know we were only filling in the holes in our soles, the way the tide rushes sand to fill in the crevices of a rocky shore. We-or maybe it was just I-bandaged our needswith what we declared was love. But, eventually, the tide draws out what it has swept in.

texto: Who can be 100 percent sure of one's choices in life? How do you know that your beloved will always remain the same, or that you'll never change your mind? Growth and change are two of the great gifts we get from time. It would be shortsighted to spurn them.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Alma Katsu que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Who can be 100 percent sure of one's choices in life? How do you know that your beloved will alwa…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, change, choices, com foco em mudanca e choices.

texto: She was telling me that I had a life of disappointment before me if I continued to love him as I did. A love that is too strong can turn poisonous and bring great unhappiness. And then, what is the remedy? Can you unlearn your heart's desire? Can you stop loving someone? Easier to drown yourself; easier to take the lover's leap.
